user_outcome: An operator's sworn maintainability review validates and reuses an exact committed report without another model dispatch, while missing, rewritten, deleted, duplicate-key, or blob-mismatched authority fails closed before lifecycle transition.
rationale: This slice owns C-05 and binds compact status authority to the full committed report rather than trusting self-described JSON. It reads only release-confined public technical evidence from committed Git objects, never the network or credentials, and never logs report prompt/diff payloads on failure. The operation is non-interactive and linear in history entries plus report bytes, with indexed uniqueness checks rather than pairwise scans.
in_scope: Define full maintainability report and compact status-entry carriers for role, phase, cycle, invocation ID, release/slice/check identity, release-confined report path, report blob OID, review base/head and fingerprint algorithm, input fingerprint, verdict, findings, and exact blocking finding IDs.

Reject duplicate raw JSON keys before normal decoding; validate raw model output against C-01's exact llm-check-report-v1 plus the committed raw-response disposition constraint that forbids engine provenance, then validate the provenance-injected persisted report against exact Baton plus the committed full overlay, while keeping Baton's closed review_scope and compact status-entry shapes byte-exact and accepting only exact 40- or 64-hex object IDs.

Resolve report_path lexically and physically beneath the reviewed slice evidence root, read the exact committed blob at the authority head, compute/compare its Git object ID, and reject working-tree-only, missing, deleted, symlinked, rewritten, or non-regular evidence.

Reject any duplicate decoded finding ID without Unicode normalization or case folding; require every blocking finding to carry structured sworn_disposition with action remediate_in_scope or re_slice and a non-empty, sorted, duplicate-free required_touchpoints array validated through C-02, forbid the member on non-blockers, derive the exact bytewise boundary union, and treat re_slice as boundary-expanding even when all named paths are already spec touchpoints.

After model validation, add the engine-owned top-level sworn object containing record_version 1, immutable scope_provenance, and the C-04 freshness_frontier; require the compact entry and committed full report to agree exactly on all Baton lifecycle and report identities while report_path/report_blob_oid pin the additive provenance outside closed Baton fields.

Derive reuse eligibility only when the exact committed full report, compact tuple, immutable C-04 semantic identity, authority head, and lifecycle-requested role/phase/cycle all match and the current moving heads pass freshness-frontier revalidation; return reuse metadata without performing or authorizing model dispatch.
Provide stable structured failures that identify the field/path class but never echo report prompt, diff, model response, credentials, or blob bytes.

Extend the real sworn maintainability review command to validate/reuse C-05 after exact C-04 scope construction; after an authoritative report identity it exits non-zero with redacted reason lifecycle_authority_unavailable until S09 lands, with zero additional dispatch or mutation.
out_of_scope: Constructing C-04 semantic scope belongs to S07; this slice consumes its fingerprint and review head as immutable inputs.
Determining whether a role/phase/cycle transition is legal belongs to S09-lifecycle-fsm-adjudication.
Writing, committing, pushing, recovering, or dispatching a report transaction belongs to S13-maintainability-engine-cutover.
Track freshness, rollback, integration readiness, and release success predicates belong to S11 and S12.

effort_complexity.rationale: Authority spans strict duplicate-safe JSON, schema validation, Git blob identity, full-versus-compact reconciliation, and exact reuse without dispatch.

risks.description: A compact status entry is trusted after its full report is rewritten or deleted.
risks.mitigation: Read only the committed report blob at the authority head and require its current object ID plus every identity field to equal the compact tuple on every use.

risks.description: Duplicate JSON keys let a validator and decoder observe different report identities.
risks.mitigation: Reject duplicates recursively from raw bytes before schema validation or typed decoding; mutation-test every authority-bearing object and map.

risks.description: Fingerprint equality alone reuses a report for the wrong role, phase, cycle, head, invocation, or stale moving frontier.
risks.mitigation: Reuse requires equality of the full C-05 tuple and immutable C-04 identity plus revalidation from the stored freshness frontier, with mismatch tests for every field and a counting dispatcher fixed at zero.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN TestReportIdentityFromCommittedHistory validates raw output and a C-05 entry/full report from a real committed repository THE SYSTEM SHALL validate raw exact Baton plus the raw-response constraint before provenance injection, validate persisted exact Baton plus the full Sworn overlay afterward, accept only exact 40- or 64-hex object IDs, and prove report path confinement, blob OID, immutable C-04 scope identity, engine provenance/freshness, lifecycle fields, verdict, dispositions, and blocking IDs all agree (N-04, N-05, N-10).

IF raw output contains engine-owned sworn provenance, violates exact Baton or the raw-response constraint, or any raw/persisted report or compact entry contains duplicate JSON keys, a 41-through-63-character object ID, any duplicate decoded finding ID, malformed disposition/touchpoints, missing/malformed provenance or freshness, malformed Baton/overlay schema, missing or rewritten blob, unsafe path, OID/verdict/blocking-ID mismatch, or any identity-field mismatch THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return non-authoritative without a reuse result.

WHEN an existing committed report's complete C-05 tuple and immutable C-04 semantic identity equal the requested review and every current moving head descends through only release-record-only or recognized disjoint synchronization history from the stored freshness frontier THE SYSTEM SHALL return that exact report as reusable and the supplied dispatch counter SHALL remain zero.

WHEN any included semantic byte, mode, object ID, scope head, immutable provenance, role, phase, cycle, or invocation identity changes THE SYSTEM SHALL refuse reuse; excluded release-record, declared-generated, exact-lockfile-only, net-zero, or later recognized disjoint synchronization changes MAY reuse only when C-04 emits identical immutable identity, frontier revalidation succeeds, and every other C-05 field still matches.

WHEN the built sworn binary runs maintainability review for an exact committed C-05 report matching C-04 THE SYSTEM SHALL reach report validation/reuse through the public command, keep the dispatch counter zero, then exit non-zero with redacted reason lifecycle_authority_unavailable and no report/status/journal/ref mutation; rewritten, missing, duplicate-key, stale, or identity-mismatched evidence SHALL fail at C-05 instead (C-04, C-05, N-04, N-05, N-10).
